revised_classification.host_catalog_pass_interpretation: Thirteen scenarios recorded Passed on macOS at commit 6460148; several passes are tautological or logical-only and do not support comparison or selection claims

transaction_behavior.original_claim: SQLite BEGIN/COMMIT transactional authority with synchronous=FULL
transaction_behavior.note: COMMIT boundary exists; proves in-process transaction completion not filesystem durability

wal_safe_duplication.original_claim: Filesystem copy of session.db as duplication
wal_safe_duplication.note: WAL enabled; copy omits -wal/-shm; duplicate content salvaged via in-memory rewrite

writer_crash_recovery.note: Token survives crash indefinitely; no takeover path

relational_model_conformance.note: Authority is single state_json blob; reclassify as SQLite-hosted JSON snapshot

append_authority.original_claim: Append-only JSONL canonical log as authority
append_authority.note: Checkpoint is authority; log records lack command payload

replay_behavior.original_claim: Log replay validates record chain and reconstructs state
replay_behavior.note: replay_log validates seq only; returns checkpoint unchanged

checkpoint_manifest_ordering.note: Log fsynced; checkpoint/manifest rename without durable sync

writer_crash_recovery.note: Token in manifest.json; no takeover implementation

compaction_recovery.original_claim: Generation rewrite with atomic directory swap
compaction_recovery.note: Post-compaction seq/log inconsistency can break recovery

mechanism_class_conformance.original_claim: Append-oriented event/log storage materially distinct from relational candidate
mechanism_class_conformance.note: Both candidates are full-state JSON snapshot persistence with different containers

supersedes_claims: Material distinctness between embedded-relational and append-bundle as declared in candidate-classes.json
Append-oriented authoritative log recovery for append-bundle candidate
Relational entity authority for embedded-relational candidate
ProcessInterruption and ProcessCrashRecovery classification for interrupted-* scenarios
Derived corruption rebuild claim for derived-state-corruption scenario
Duplication independence claim for semantic-duplication scenario
FilesystemDurability and HardwarePowerLoss for spike-v1 macOS run
Cross-platform eligibility beyond macOS host execution

preserved_original_evidence.policy: Original spike-v1-macos-6460148 artifacts retained unchanged; this reclassification adds explicit downgrade without deletion
